title: How a Radar Satellite Tracks Russian Attacks on Ukraine's Power Grid
subtitle: When the lights go out, satellites are watching
blurb: Radar from space can see through clouds and darkness to the moment a power plant is hit. A look at how open satellite data lays bare the war on Ukraine's grid.

title: The Cyclone Harry on the Central Mediterranean Migration Route
subtitle: When the weather and the crossings collide
blurb: A storm sweeps the Central Mediterranean just as the boats push off from Libya. The story of the night the sea turned, rebuilt hour by hour.

title: Tracking Algerian Military Activity
subtitle: The dataset nobody was meant to have
blurb: Algeria won't share its migration figures — so its own army's weekly bulletins became the source. Inside the making of a record built from scratch.

title: The Caucasus Corridor
subtitle: Where the planes go when the sky closes
blurb: After Russia shut its airspace, Europe's flights to Asia found a narrow new path through the Caucasus — shadowed by the GPS jamming that now follows them.

title: How Does an Election Forecast Model Work?
subtitle: Everyone cites the forecast. Few know what's inside.
blurb: Open up an election model and watch it work, from raw polls to a seat-by-seat call. No statistics degree required.

title: U.S. Territorial Expansion and Native American Land Loss
subtitle: Watch a continent change hands
blurb: A moving map of how Native nations were pushed off their land as the United States grew — and a quiet challenge to the story America tells about it.

title: Don't Trust the Polls. Don't Ignore Them Either.
subtitle: Not crystal balls. Not noise, either.
blurb: Five things almost everyone gets wrong about election polls, cleared up with the numbers from Germany's last vote.

title: The Changing Face of Parliament
subtitle: Who actually sits in the Bundestag?
blurb: Seventy-five years of German politics told through the people in the room — their age, gender, faith, and where they came from.

title: The Geography of Silence — Press Freedom in Türkiye 2015–2025
subtitle: Where the press went quiet
blurb: A decade of journalists detained, sued, and silenced across Türkiye, plotted on a single map. The geography of a slow crackdown.

title: The Parallel Record: Eagle Hills Georgia Ad Investigation
subtitle: Reading between the languages
blurb: A glossy developer sells a Georgian dream in English — then quietly resells it in Russian. What thousands of ads reveal about who's really being courted.

title: Spring on Two Wheels
subtitle: Easier riding, deadlier roads
blurb: E-bikes put cycling within everyone's reach. So why aren't cyclists any safer? A spring-season look at the gap.

title: Heat-Stressed India: The Seen and the Unseen
subtitle: Where the heat hits hardest
blurb: Across India, the fiercest heat lands on the mothers and children least able to withstand it. Mapping the districts where the danger doubles up.

title: From Ice Cream to Politics: How Opinions Become Numbers
subtitle: It starts with ice cream
blurb: Answer a few questions about your favourite flavour and you'll never read an election poll the same way again. A playful guide to how opinions become numbers.

title: Paper Money: How Not to Store Value
subtitle: Watch your savings quietly melt
blurb: Tuck cash under the mattress and time eats it alive. A scroll-driven essay on why money is such a bad place to store value.

title: The Efficiency Trap
subtitle: The world runs through a few narrow gates
blurb: From the Strait of Hormuz to a single rare-earth town, modern supply chains lean on a handful of chokepoints. What happens when one shuts?

title: Price Convergence in Europe
subtitle: The East is catching up — at a price
blurb: Prices across Central and Eastern Europe are converging with the West. The open question is whether wages are keeping pace.

title: Are Data Centres Driving Up Your Electricity Bill?
subtitle: The cloud has to live somewhere
blurb: Every search and stream runs through a data centre drawing serious power. In Virginia, a test of whether your electricity bill is footing it.

title: Food Deserts in the United States
subtitle: A line drawn in the 1930s you can still taste
blurb: Trace a redlining map from nearly a century ago and it still predicts where Americans struggle to buy fresh food today.

title: Remigrazione: How a Word Entered Italian Parliament
subtitle: From the chat rooms to the chamber
blurb: A word born on the far-right fringe now echoes through Italy's parliament. Tracing its climb, one mention at a time.

title: Russia's Systematic Targeting of Ukraine's Power Grid
subtitle: Seven waves against one grid
blurb: Mapping how Russia tried to switch off Ukraine — strike by strike, substation by substation.

title: Berlin's First of May: A Map of Displacement
subtitle: The march is a map of displacement
blurb: Follow Berlin's May Day route and you're really tracing forty years of who got priced out of the city.

title: The Invisible Battlefield: GPS Interference from Space
subtitle: Somewhere up there, GPS goes dark
blurb: An invisible kind of interference is spreading across the world's conflict zones. Mapping where the signal drops — and why.

title: Berlin's Emergency Response: Your Neighborhood in Numbers
subtitle: How fast would help reach your street?
blurb: Type in your Berlin neighbourhood and see how quickly the fire brigade would arrive — and how that stacks up against the rest of the city.

title: The Attention Economy of Comedy Scandals
subtitle: Does the scandal stick, or the joke?
blurb: When a comedian lands in the headlines, what kind of fame lasts? A trip through the traffic spikes on Wikipedia.

title: Algeria's Migration Enforcement: Traces in Military Reports
subtitle: The arrests Europe never sees
blurb: They're printed every week in Algeria's own military bulletins. You just have to know where to look.

title: America's Silent Killer: Heat Deaths in the United States
subtitle: The disaster that doesn't make the news
blurb: Heat kills more Americans than hurricanes, floods, and tornadoes combined — but only if you count the deaths the official toll leaves out.
